house-yard.context: Reolink E1 Outdoor Pro 4K PTZ camera (192.168.0.88); currently aimed at the yard, sky, and coop approach

usb-cam.context: Generic USB webcam (UVC 1920x1080), plugged into the GWTC Gateway laptop at 192.168.0.68 and served on port 8089 by a Windows scheduled task ('usb-cam-host', running interactively as the 'cam' autologon user, C:\farm-services\usb-cam-host\start.bat). 24-Apr-2026: moved from the Mac Mini to GWTC because the Mini's heat-lamp-lit brooder angle red-clipped the sensor and produced unusable frames; the GWTC coop run is natural-daylight and this cheap UVC sensor handles it cleanly (1920x1080 full color). device_index=1 on the Windows side because device 0 is GWTC's built-in Hy-HD-Camera already held by MediaMTX for the 'gwtc' RTSP path.

s7-cam.context: Samsung Galaxy S7 running IP Webcam app, plugged into a USB wall brick for power (untethered from MBA 26-Apr-2026, v2.37.8). 26-Apr-2026 v2.37.10: switched FROM /photoaf.jpg TO /photo.jpg + explicit /focus + 2s wait. /photoaf.jpg's built-in AF was running concurrently with our /focus call and the two were interfering — frames came back rated 'soft' even with continuous-picture mode active. One AF cycle (explicit /focus, sleep 2s, fetch /photo.jpg) is more reliable than two. Continuous-picture AF + incandescent WB set at Guardian startup (v2.27.7). Sony IMX260 sensor, 1920x1080, f/1.7. Best-quality source in the fleet — aimed at the NESTING BOX (2026-04-30 onward). Cadence dropped to 3s and priority=1 so when the VLM slot opens, s7 wins ties. 2026-04-30: moved from brooder to the NESTING BOX.

gwtc.context: Gateway laptop (Windows 11, 192.168.0.68) built-in webcam streamed via ffmpeg+MediaMTX; 1280x720, fixed focus, 2026-04-30: moved to the ROOF OF THE COOP for an overhead/approach view. Previously was in the coop run interior. Pipeline pulls Guardian's snapshot API rather than RTSP direct — Guardian's ring buffer rides through the dshow-zombie windows that farmcam-watchdog takes ~90s to clear. Burst-of-3 with median-selection dodges the transient H.264 decode artifacts Boss sees after the laptop gets jostled (vertical stripes, smearing).

mba-cam.context: MacBook Air 2013 FaceTime HD camera (1280x720), served at 192.168.0.50:8089 via usb-cam-host (com.farmguardian.usb-cam-host LaunchAgent on the MBA). Optional brooder monitor — loading/unloading that LaunchAgent is the on/off switch; no config change needed. Currently watching the brooder with chicks. device_index=0 on MBA = FaceTime HD (USB cam is on GWTC, so FaceTime is the only camera on this box).
